feed.title: [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets

entry.title: [BIP Proposal] Hybrid SPHINCS+ / secp256k1 Key Derivation for Quantum-Resistant Paper Wallets
entry.summary:
The message raises significant concerns regarding a proposal labeled as SPX-QEC which appears to be associated with the creation of secp256k1 keypairs purportedly enhanced to resist quantum attacks. The sender criticizes the lack of clarity and coherence in the documentation provided, mentioning that the terminology used resembles "word-salad" and expresses skepticism about the ability to "quantum harden" secp256k1 keypairs through altering entropy. They highlight that due to the immutable nature of curve points, Shor's algorithm could still effectively compute the discrete logarithm of any point on the curve, thus questioning the effectiveness of the proposed method.
Further, the authenticity and safety of the tool mentioned for generating paper wallet keys are questioned. The sender describes the key generation code as "unreadable gibberish," suggesting potential risks where the output entropy might be manipulated by the author, leading to possible theft of funds stored using the generated keys. A stark warning is issued against clicking on unverified links, implying a risk of phishing or other fraudulent activities.
The sender requests more comprehensive explanations and proper documentation of the code to assess the viability and security of the proposal adequately. This caution reflects a broader concern for security practices within the cryptocurrency development community, particularly on platforms like the Bitcoin Development Mailing List where such topics are of paramount importance.
